title: Visibility trigger calls SECURITY-DEFINER helper that is REVOKEd from service_role — breaks all direct service_role INSERTs to match_decisions
summary: `_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(UUID, UUID)` is REVOKEd from PUBLIC, anon, authenticated, AND service_role (line 104). The BEFORE-INSERT trigger function `_match_decisions_visibility_check()` (NOT SECURITY DEFINER) fires for every INSERT on match_decisions and PERFORMs the helper. When service_role inserts directly (e.g. test suites in src/__tests__/match-decisions-xor-rls.test.ts and src/__tests__/bridge-outcome-cron.test.ts use `admin.from('match_decisions').insert(...)` via service_role key), the trigger runs as service_role and the inner CALL to the SECURITY-DEFINER helper is subject to standard EXECUTE-privilege checks against the calling role — which has been explicitly REVOKEd. Result: every direct service_role INSERT into match_decisions with kind in ('voluntary_add','bridge_recommended') and a non-null strategy_id will fail with `42501 permission denied for function _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator` instead of either succeeding or returning the intended 42501 visibility error. SECURITY-DEFINER on the helper does not bypass EXECUTE privilege at the call-site — it only switches role inside the body.
evidence: Line 104: `REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(UUID, UUID) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated, service_role;` Line 114-134: trigger function is plain LANGUAGE plpgsql (no SECURITY DEFINER). Line 125: `IF NOT public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(NEW.strategy_id, NEW.allocator_id) THEN`. Compare with the legitimate-only-from-migration helpers `_assert_no_public_execute` (mig 134) and `_assert_retention_columns` (mig 160200) that are also REVOKEd from service_role but are NEVER called from a runtime trigger path. Tests src/__tests__/match-decisions-xor-rls.test.ts L117-L218 and src/__tests__/bridge-outcome-cron.test.ts L133-L143 do direct admin.from('match_decisions').insert(...) and will start failing once this migration applies.
fix: Either (a) GRANT EXECUTE on `_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator` to service_role (and authenticated, if admins need direct INSERT via the match_dec_admin_all RLS policy), or (b) mark the trigger function `_match_decisions_visibility_check()` as SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = public, pg_catalog and set the function owner to postgres so the inner helper call runs under the owner's privileges. Option (b) is more conservative because it does not widen the helper's EXECUTE surface beyond superuser-equivalent migrations.

title: Tightened voluntary_modify CHECK enforces the exact row shape the cron holding-branch attributes to — header's stated 'closes mis-attribution' is misleading; cron coverage is unchanged
summary: Migration header explicitly states: 'the holding-branch in compute_bridge_outcome_deltas filters on `md.original_strategy_id IS NULL AND md.original_holding_ref IS NOT NULL` — exactly the voluntary_modify shape when original_strategy_id IS NULL. So voluntary_modify with NULL original_strategy_id is silently picked up by the holding branch (incorrect attribution).' The mitigation TIGHTENS the CHECK to `original_strategy_id IS NULL` for all voluntary_modify rows, which means EVERY voluntary_modify row now matches the holding-branch's exact filter shape. The migration therefore makes the alleged mis-attribution universal, not eliminated. The coverage map claims H-0957/H-0961/H-0963 are closed — but only the CHECK-shape invariant is closed; the cron mis-attribution is left intact (or made worse). If the cron behavior is intentional (voluntary_modify SHOULD be tracked via the holding branch), then the header narrative is wrong; if it is unintentional, the cron body needs to filter out kind='voluntary_modify' decisions.
evidence: Mig 080 L244-L251 (holding branch): `JOIN public.match_decisions md ON md.id = bo.match_decision_id ... WHERE bo.kind = 'allocated' AND md.original_strategy_id IS NULL AND md.original_holding_ref IS NOT NULL AND hp.symbol IS NOT NULL`. After 20260516160500, the v2 CHECK on voluntary_modify is `original_holding_ref IS NOT NULL AND strategy_id IS NULL AND original_strategy_id IS NULL` — a strict subset of the holding-branch's filter. commit_scenario_batch (mig 20260515210400 L382-L392) writes a bridge_outcomes row with kind='allocated' for voluntary_modify, so cron picks it up.
fix: Either (a) add a `AND (md.kind IS NULL OR md.kind <> 'voluntary_modify')` filter to the holding branch of compute_bridge_outcome_deltas in a follow-up migration if mis-attribution is real, or (b) update the migration header to clarify that the CHECK closes the SHAPE invariant only and that cron coverage for voluntary_modify is intentional and tracked via the holding branch.

title: percent_allocated numeric cast in _validate_scenario_diff is unguarded — non-numeric input raises 22P02 with no diff-index annotation, defeating the migration's stated DX goal
summary: The migration header advertises 'raises structured RAISE EXCEPTION (22023 invalid_parameter_value) with a clear error message including the per-diff position'. The strategy_id UUID cast IS protected by an EXCEPTION WHEN invalid_text_representation block (L93-L98) that re-raises 22023 with the diff index. The percent_allocated numeric casts (L103, L118, L144) are bare casts with no protective BEGIN/EXCEPTION. A diff like `{"kind":"voluntary_add","strategy_id":"<uuid>","percent_allocated":"not-a-number"}` would raise SQLSTATE 22P02 'invalid input syntax for type numeric: not-a-number' WITHOUT the per-diff index annotation. This is an inconsistency: structured handling for UUID, raw error for numeric. The result is the helper's value proposition (clean per-diff index pointers) regresses to the same hard-to-debug error mode the migration claims to fix.
evidence: L93-L98: `BEGIN PERFORM v_strat::uuid; EXCEPTION WHEN invalid_text_representation THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'commit_scenario_batch[index=%]: strategy_id=% is not a valid UUID' ... USING ERRCODE = '22023'; END;` L103: `v_pct := (p_diff->>'percent_allocated')::numeric;` — bare cast, no EXCEPTION block.
fix: Wrap each numeric cast in the same BEGIN/EXCEPTION pattern: `BEGIN v_pct := (p_diff->>'percent_allocated')::numeric; EXCEPTION WHEN invalid_text_representation THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'commit_scenario_batch[index=%]: percent_allocated=% is not a valid number', p_index, p_diff->>'percent_allocated' USING ERRCODE = '22023'; END;`

title: _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator may see RLS-filtered strategies row when run as a non-BYPASSRLS function owner
summary: The helper is SECURITY DEFINER but `strategies` has RLS enabled (mig 20260405061912 L5) with policy `strategies_read` USING `status = 'published' OR user_id = auth.uid()`. SECURITY DEFINER switches role to the function owner but does NOT bypass RLS — only the BYPASSRLS role attribute or `ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` bypasses it. If the function owner (postgres in Supabase Cloud usually) lacks BYPASSRLS, the `SELECT organization_id FROM strategies WHERE id = p_strategy_id` may return zero rows for an unpublished, non-owned strategy → v_org_id stays NULL → helper returns TRUE (treats as globally visible). This silently lets an org-scoped, unpublished strategy slip the visibility gate. The risk is highest in self-hosted Supabase or CI where postgres may not have BYPASSRLS.
evidence: Mig 20260405061912 L5: `ALTER TABLE strategies ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;` L28-L30: `CREATE POLICY strategies_read ON strategies FOR SELECT USING (status = 'published' OR user_id = auth.uid());` Mig 20260516160700 L66-L68: `SELECT organization_id INTO v_org_id FROM strategies WHERE id = p_strategy_id;` No `SET LOCAL row_security = off;` or FORCE bypass.
fix: Add `SET LOCAL row_security = off;` at the top of the function body, OR explicitly `ALTER FUNCTION public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(UUID, UUID) OWNER TO postgres;` after CREATE (so it inherits the migration-runner's BYPASSRLS), OR add a self-verifying probe in the DO block (L154-L184) that asserts the helper sees a known-unpublished strategy correctly (returns FALSE for org-scoped + non-member).

title: M-0822 behavioral CTE probe greps function source — block-comments not stripped, brittle to future C-style comment additions
summary: The probe strips line comments with `regexp_replace(v_body, '--[^\n]*', '', 'g')` but does NOT strip /* ... */ block comments. The mitigation's stated goal is 'comment retention alone would not satisfy both probes' — but a future maintainer adding a /* voluntary_add_candidates AS ( */ block comment inside compute_bridge_outcome_deltas (e.g., a TODO note or legacy code reference) would trivially satisfy both regexes without any live CTE. Current mig 080 body uses only line comments so the probe is correct today; the brittleness is in resisting future drift, not current correctness.
evidence: L129: `v_body_stripped := regexp_replace(v_body, '--[^\n]*', '', 'g');` L135: `IF v_body_stripped !~* 'voluntary_add_candidates\s+AS\s*\(' THEN` — pattern matches inside block-comment text too. L140: same shape.
fix: Extend the strip to also remove block comments: `v_body_stripped := regexp_replace(regexp_replace(v_body, '/\*.*?\*/', '', 'g'), '--[^\n]*', '', 'g');` (the first regex needs the 's' flag for multiline, or replace with a non-greedy match across newlines). Alternatively, replace the substring probe with an EXPLAIN (FORMAT JSON) call against a no-op invocation of the function and assert the plan contains the CTE node — more robust but heavier.

title: Tightening voluntary_modify CHECK without a code-path probe for mig 080's compute_bridge_outcome_deltas voluntary_modify support — silent gap if cron lacks a voluntary_modify CTE branch
summary: Mig 080 added explicit CTE branches for strategy / holding / voluntary_add. There is no voluntary_modify CTE branch (the holding branch coincidentally matches its shape — see finding above). Mig 160500 enforces the row shape but does NOT probe pg_get_functiondef for a voluntary_modify branch the way mig 160300 STEP 3 probes for voluntary_add_candidates. If a future fix splits voluntary_modify into its own CTE branch and someone deletes/renames it, the cron silently stops processing those rows and no migration-time gate catches it.
evidence: Mig 160300 L107-L144 has a behavioral CTE probe for voluntary_add. Mig 160500 has no equivalent probe for voluntary_modify — only the constraint check (L98-L117). The coverage map at L114-L115 claims H-0961 (silent-failure-hunter c8) is mitigated by the CHECK tightening; but a silent-failure probe should also gate the cron's handling of the kind.
fix: Either (a) add a behavioral probe in mig 160500 STEP 3 that asserts compute_bridge_outcome_deltas pg_get_functiondef references voluntary_modify in a live (non-comment) context, OR (b) document in the migration header that voluntary_modify cron coverage is intentional via the holding branch and add an SQL test (src/__tests__/bridge-outcome-cron-voluntary-modify.test.ts) verifying that voluntary_modify decisions get delta_30d computed.

title: Trigger function _match_decisions_visibility_check lacks SET search_path — depends on caller's search_path for the public.* call
summary: `_match_decisions_visibility_check()` is LANGUAGE plpgsql without `SET search_path = public, pg_catalog`. The only schema-qualified reference is `public._assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator(...)` (line 125) which is fully-qualified — so the missing search_path is not exploitable for hijacking. But the convention used by every other audit-2026-05-07 SECDEF/trigger function in this codebase (sanitize_user, commit_scenario_batch, retention crons) is to set search_path defensively even on plain plpgsql functions. The grok-adversarial override note (sql-migrations-coverage-2026-05-16.md L175-L186) cites this exact convention. Inconsistency with the codebase norm.
evidence: L114-L117: `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public._match_decisions_visibility_check() RETURNS TRIGGER LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $trg$` — no `SET search_path`. Compare to `_assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator` at L41-L49 which DOES include `SET search_path = public, pg_catalog`.
fix: Add `SET search_path = public, pg_catalog` between LANGUAGE plpgsql and AS $trg$. Matches the codebase convention and removes any ambiguity if a future maintainer adds an unqualified reference inside the function body.

title: Drop-DEFAULT migration's NOT-NULL contract assertion races future schema changes — verifying via information_schema after the same-tx DDL is safe but the NOT-NULL probe is read-only and won't refuse a divergent state
summary: STEP 3 verifies `is_nullable = 'NO'` for match_decisions.kind. If a future drift removes the NOT NULL (e.g., a manual hotfix or a wrong-direction migration), this PR's apply would catch it because STEP 1 doesn't refuse on NOT NULL absence — it only checks DEFAULT state. But STEP 1's pre-flight does NOT assert NOT NULL is present, only that no NULL rows exist. If somehow the column got DROP NOT NULL but happens to have zero NULL rows at apply time, the migration completes successfully and the post-drop INSERT-without-kind would no longer fail loudly. Minor — the assertion at STEP 3 raises afterward, which is good — but the pre-flight on STEP 1 could have caught it before the DDL.
evidence: STEP 1 L33-L40 checks only `COUNT(*) FROM match_decisions WHERE kind IS NULL` and DEFAULT presence — no probe of `is_nullable`. STEP 3 L84-L92 probes is_nullable AFTER the DROP DEFAULT.
fix: Move the NOT NULL probe (or an additional `is_nullable = 'NO'` precondition check) to STEP 1, raising EXCEPTION before the DROP DEFAULT fires. The current STEP 3 verifying probe is necessary but a STEP 1 pre-flight provides safer abort semantics — operator can investigate without partial-apply rollback.

title: _assert_retention_columns checks only retention-relevant columns — fails to gate api_key_rotation_reminder cron's other dependencies (profiles.id, api_keys.user_id, api_keys.created_at)
summary: The helper probes 6 columns: api_keys.is_active, profiles.email, and 4 columns on notification_dispatches. The mig 056 docstring (L4-L13) explicitly cites api_key_rotation_reminder cron as referencing api_keys.is_active AND profiles.email — but that cron body likely also references api_keys.user_id (to FK-join profiles), api_keys.created_at (for the >90d rotation threshold), api_keys.id, profiles.id. If any of those drift (rename, drop), the cron breaks silently at next tick and this probe doesn't catch it. The mitigation header at L4-L13 says 'schema drift (rename, fork, hotfix) silently breaks the cron body every night' but only catches a partial column set.
evidence: L49-L56: probe lists 6 columns. The actual cron bodies in mig 056 / retention_crons_high_hardening also reference api_keys.user_id, api_keys.created_at, api_keys.id, profiles.id — none of which are in the assertion array.
fix: Expand the probe array to include all columns the retention crons reference, derived by grepping the cron body in mig 056 + 20260515210200 + mig 121: at minimum add 'public.api_keys.user_id', 'public.api_keys.created_at', 'public.api_keys.id', 'public.profiles.id'. Better: regex-extract column references from cron.job.command at apply time and assert each exists. Same approach for the other 4 retention cron jobs (audit_log_cold, scenario_commit_idempotency, etc.) if they reference distinct columns.

title: sanitize_user verification probe relies on substring matches inside stripped pg_get_functiondef — false-pass surface if comments retain key strings
summary: STEP 2 verification uses `v_body_stripped NOT LIKE '%pg_advisory_xact_lock%'` and similar substring probes. The line-comment strip via regexp_replace at L253 removes `--` comments but NOT `/* ... */` block comments. A future revision that moves `pg_advisory_xact_lock` into a comment block (e.g., 'NOTE: pg_advisory_xact_lock was replaced by row-level lock here') and removes the live call would silently pass the verification. Same risk as M-0822 finding in mig 160300 — the audit migration explicitly aims for stronger gates than mig 080's substring probes, but inherits the same C-style comment hole.
evidence: L253: `v_body_stripped := regexp_replace(v_body, '--[^\n]*', '', 'g');` (line comments only). L261: `IF v_body_stripped NOT LIKE '%pg_advisory_xact_lock%' THEN`. The regex `!~* 'DELETE\s+FROM\s+notification_dispatches\s+WHERE\s+recipient_email'` (L256) is shape-aware (not just substring) — better. The plain `NOT LIKE '%...%'` probes at L261, L267, L270, L273 inherit the comment-block hole.
fix: Either upgrade the `NOT LIKE` probes to shape-aware regexes that pin specific call/statement contexts (e.g. `!~* 'PERFORM\s+pg_advisory_xact_lock\s*\('`), OR also strip block comments from v_body. Pattern parallels the M-0822 fix recommendation in 160300.
